CweFindings.Name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
CweFindings.Description: The product specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors.
CweFindings.Explanation: In application.properties at line 17, `management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=*` exposes all Spring Boot Actuator endpoints (including /actuator/env, /actuator/heapdump, /actuator/beans, /actuator/loggers, etc.) to unauthenticated access. No Spring Security configuration is present in the application, meaning any actor with network access can read sensitive runtime configuration, heap dumps, and environment variables.

CweFindings.Name: Insufficient Logging
CweFindings.Description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
CweFindings.Explanation: No logging (no Logger, @Slf4j, or LoggerFactory usage) exists in any controller or service class. Security-critical events such as data creation, modification, and deletion (owners, pets, visits) are performed without any audit trail. The absence of logging makes it impossible to detect or investigate unauthorized access or data manipulation.
